text: Choosing adequate stimuli for eliciting specific emotional states is an essential issue in affective science. Most of the available databases of affective stimuli are available only for a restricted set of stimuli modalities: few databases are available for auditory and language stimuli while even fewer are available for visual stimuli; however, many studies have been based on visual stimuli and on auditory stimuli where the affective value has to be attributed to the associated language, therefore a large amount of studies exploit a limited set of stimulus materials both in behavioral and in neuroimaging studies. While, many potential problems have been highlighted regarding the few available databases, here we deal with a new possibility: we here introduce the Nencki Affective Picture System (NAPS), a database of high-quality affective images, which comprises 1356 photographs belonging to five classes: people, faces, animals, objects, landscapes. All images were rated for valence, arousal and for approach-avoidance using computerized bipolar semantic slider scales by a group of 204 European subjects. We here present the average affective ratings for each one of the five classes of stimuli for the three emotional dimensions: valence, arousal and approach-avoidance. These ratings are validated by using ratings derived from different procedures and provided in the text for references to the Self-Assessment Manikin and to the International Affective Picture System. The physical properties of the images (luminance, contrast, entropy values) are reported. The NAPS and the related ratings and image parameters can be exploited for selecting sets of affective visual stimuli suitable for studying the effect of a large number of specific variables in experimental investigations, according to the specific research hypotheses dealt with by the experimenter. The NAPS is provided at no cost, for noncommercial purposes, by request at http://naps.nencki.gov.pl .
